HPBR group had a significantly stronger grip
strength than the control group (P = 0.04, one-way
ANOVA; P < 0.05 control group vs. HPBR group,
Dunnett’s post-test, Fig. 4b). The results suggest that
HPBR ameliorates aging-related motor dysfunction.
HPBR reduces the amount of Aβ1–42 in the SAMP8
mice brain
To clarify the mechanism of improved cognition in
SAMP8 mice by HPBR, we measured the amount of Aβ
in the mice brain after HPBR administration (Fig. 5).
The amount of Aβ1–42 in the HPBR group (235 ±
9 pmol/g of tissue) was significantly less than those in
the control (272 ± 5 pmol/g of tissue) and PR groups
